ExactSource: Super 3-D Noah's Ark MIDI-to-AdLib rhythm-mode playback
Warning: The WAV previews are exact to the Noah rhythm-mode code path. The OP2 bank is a best-effort export for IMFCreator-style tooling because OP2 is not a perfect container for Noah's hardcoded rhythm operators.

DrumMappings.Name: Noah Bass Drum
DrumMappings.ExactOrigin: Bass drum is exact two-operator channel 6 rhythm voice from instrument[9].
DrumMappings.ApproximationNote: OP2 export is structurally faithful for the bass drum because Noah uses a full two-operator rhythm voice here.

DrumMappings.Name: Noah Snare
DrumMappings.ExactOrigin: Snare uses the carrier-side rhythm operator configured from instrument[11].
DrumMappings.ApproximationNote: OP2 export is an approximation because Noah's snare is a rhythm-mode single-operator voice, not a standalone two-operator patch.

DrumMappings.Name: Noah Tom
DrumMappings.ExactOrigin: Tom uses the modulator-side rhythm operator configured from instrument[12].
DrumMappings.ApproximationNote: OP2 export is an approximation because Noah's tom is a rhythm-mode single-operator voice, not a standalone two-operator patch.

DrumMappings.Name: Noah Hi-Hat
DrumMappings.ExactOrigin: Hi-hat uses the modulator-side rhythm operator configured from instrument[10].
DrumMappings.ApproximationNote: OP2 export is an approximation because Noah's hi-hat is a rhythm-mode single-operator voice, not a standalone two-operator patch.
